project.name: clinic-app
project.description: A clinic management application built with React, TypeScript, and Vite, packaged as a desktop app using Electron, and integrated with Supabase and TailwindCSS. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Main container component of the React client, loading all other views, routing navigation, maintaining dark mode state, managing toasts, and rendering layout wrapper.

nodes.name: Assessment.tsx
nodes.summary: A tabbed component for creating, viewing, updating, and deleting clinical assessments for patients, including doctor assignment and assessment details.

nodes.name: CommandPalette.tsx
nodes.summary: A keyboard-accessible modal overlay that allows quick navigation and patient searching across the clinic application.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: The bootstrap file for the React application that mounts the App component onto the HTML root element.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Unified TypeScript type definitions and interfaces for clinic models such as User, Doctor, Client, Appointment, and Finance records.

nodes.name: AIView.tsx
nodes.summary: A dashboard view providing an AI assistant interface where clinical staff can ask questions relating to patients and treatment records.

nodes.name: AttendanceView.tsx
nodes.summary: Management view for clinical users to clock in and out, tracking daily shifts, arrival/departure logs, and daily status.

nodes.name: BranchSelectorView.tsx
nodes.summary: Selection view for choosing the active clinic branch upon initial login, pulling available branches from the database.

nodes.name: CalendarView.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive interactive scheduling view that displays clinical appointments hourly, with CRUD actions and completion notes.

nodes.name: ClientsView.tsx
nodes.summary: Patient directory view with search filters, sorting, profile classification, and options to add new clients.

nodes.name: DashboardView.tsx
nodes.summary: Core clinic dashboard rendering summary statistics, pain alert trackers, today's schedule, active staff sessions, and network URLs.

nodes.name: DoctorsView.tsx
nodes.summary: Management view for administrative users to add, update, and toggle active status of clinical doctors.

nodes.name: FinanceManagementView.tsx
nodes.summary: Administrative dashboard managing salaries, monthly revenue breakdowns, staff loans, clinic waste logs, and billing types.

nodes.name: LoginView.tsx
nodes.summary: Authentication screen validating user credentials via the Electron IPC API to establish active clinic sessions.

nodes.name: ReportsView.tsx
nodes.summary: Administrative reporting page aggregating payments, sessions, waste, loans, and shift durations for custom date ranges and doctors.

nodes.name: SetupView.tsx
nodes.summary: Initial admin configuration wizard to establish the database and first admin account when no users exist.

nodes.name: window.d.ts
nodes.summary: Global TypeScript declarations exposing the Electron IPC channel APIs via the custom window.api object.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: The main container component of the React client, loading all other views, routing navigation, maintaining dark mode state, managing toasts, and rendering layout wrapper.

nodes.summary: Renders lists of staff arrival/departure status and buttons for manual clock-in/out overrides.

nodes.name: DashboardView

nodes.name: DoctorsView
nodes.summary: Master-detail administration dashboard for clinical therapists and active flags.

nodes.name: FinanceManagementView
nodes.summary: Comprehensive administrative system for salaries, loans, waste costs, and billing codes.

nodes.name: LoginView
nodes.summary: UI containing inputs for username and password with submission handlers checking active logins.

nodes.name: database.js
nodes.summary: Initializes and manages the SQLite database (using sql.js), sets up the tables/schema, handles migrations, seeds initial data, and exports helper methods for querying and database persistence.

nodes.name: getDatabase
nodes.summary: Resolves the database path from the config or defaults, initializes the sql.js database, sets up debounced atomic writes to disk and on-quit persistence, and returns a wrapped Database-like interface.

nodes.name: initSchema
nodes.summary: Creates all application tables and indexes, handles database migrations for newer versions, and seeds default exercise regions, exercises, and clinical assessment tests.

nodes.name: reloadDatabase
nodes.summary: Resets the current database instance reference and re-initializes/re-fetches the database.

nodes.name: logger.js
nodes.summary: A logging utility for Electron that writes JSON-formatted log entries to a local log file, with automatic log rotation support when the file size exceeds a threshold.

nodes.name: getLogFilePath
nodes.summary: Resolves the target file path for logging, defaulting to the Electron userData directory or falling back to a relative project directory.

nodes.name: rotateLogsIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Checks if the log file size exceeds 5MB, and if so, rotates existing log files by renaming them sequentially.

nodes.name: writeLog
nodes.summary: Triggers log rotation, formats the message, log level, module name, and metadata into a JSON line, and appends it to the log file.

nodes.name: sync.js
nodes.summary: Handles synchronization of local SQLite database records (clients, sessions, clinical findings, payments, user accounts, and appointments) with Supabase Cloud databases.

nodes.name: syncToCloud
nodes.summary: Pushes an array of local records to a specified Supabase cloud table using an upsert operation.

nodes.name: syncPatientPlan
nodes.summary: Processes and uploads a detailed payload of patient-related data, including profiles, home and clinical exercises, sessions, payments, physical therapy red flags, and subjective assessments to cloud tables.

nodes.name: syncAppointments
nodes.summary: Fetches local appointments, cleans up deleted ones on the remote Supabase database, and upserts current appointments to the portal.

nodes.name: syncUsers
nodes.summary: Synchronizes the users database table with the remote portal_users Supabase table, cleaning up deleted local users.

nodes.name: processSyncQueue
nodes.summary: Drains the local offline sync queue, retrying failed sync payloads up to 5 times and updating task statuses.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: The main process file for the Electron application. It sets up the IPC bridge, manages in-memory active user sessions, exposes SQLite query handlers to the renderer, spins up a local server for static files and mobile uploads, and schedules automated background archiving and cloud synchronization jobs.

nodes.name: getLocalIp
nodes.summary: Retrieves the local IPv4 address by inspecting network interfaces, prioritizing Ethernet and Wi-Fi networks.

nodes.name: getTailscaleIp
nodes.summary: Detects and returns the IP address belonging to the Tailscale VPN network interface, if active.

nodes.name: startLocalServer
nodes.summary: Starts an HTTP server to serve static assets, media files, and receive client document uploads from mobile clients.

nodes.name: createWindow
nodes.summary: Creates the main BrowserWindow, configures web preferences, loads the index file, and manages window lifecycle.

nodes.name: runAutoArchiveJob
nodes.summary: Deactivates/archives clients who have been inactive (no appointments, payments, or exercises assigned) for more than 28 days.

nodes.name: syncPackageSessions
nodes.summary: Recalculates and updates the number of sessions used from prepaid packages based on the user's logged sessions.

nodes.name: runBackgroundSyncJob
nodes.summary: Compiles local changes for patients with sync tokens and runs the cloud sync process, queueing failed operations in SyncQueue.

nodes.name: core.py
nodes.summary: Implements the BM25 search logic and domain detection for querying style, color, chart, landing page, and typography CSV datasets.

nodes.name: BM25
nodes.summary: A class implementing the BM25 ranking algorithm, including tokenization, term-frequency calculation, inverse document frequency (IDF) indexing, and query scoring.

nodes.name: _load_csv
nodes.summary: Helper function that opens a CSV file and reads it into a list of dictionaries.

nodes.name: _search_csv
nodes.summary: Performs BM25 search over a CSV file's search columns and returns filtered results above a score of 0.

nodes.name: detect_domain
nodes.summary: Heuristically detects the most relevant design system domain from keyword matches in the search query.

nodes.name: search
nodes.summary: Core entry point to search domain guidelines, auto-detecting the domain if none is provided.

nodes.name: search_stack
nodes.summary: Performs stack-specific guideline search using BM25 indexing against localized stack CSV datasets.

nodes.name: design_system.py
nodes.summary: Generates comprehensive design system configurations (Master specifications and page overrides) by cross-referencing CSV queries and reasoning rules.

nodes.name: DesignSystemGenerator
nodes.summary: Coordinates multi-domain searches and applies design reasoning rules to resolve color, layout, style, and typography recommendations.

nodes.name: format_ascii_box
nodes.summary: Formats the resolved design system parameters into a stylized ASCII box representation for CLI rendering.

nodes.name: format_markdown
nodes.summary: Formats design system parameters into a structured Markdown document.

nodes.name: generate_design_system
nodes.summary: High-level entry point to invoke the design system generator and format the output based on options.

nodes.name: persist_design_system
nodes.summary: Saves generated design system MASTER.md and optional page override files to the project design-system directory.

nodes.name: format_master_md
nodes.summary: Constructs the MASTER.md file content, outlining global rules, color palettes, typography, spacing, shadows, component specifications, and checklist.

nodes.name: format_page_override_md
nodes.summary: Constructs page-specific override Markdown files specifying layout, spacing, and typography deviations from the master guide.

nodes.name: _generate_intelligent_overrides
nodes.summary: Queries style, UX, and landing page CSV rules for a specific page type to resolve layout and spacing overrides.

nodes.name: _detect_page_type
nodes.summary: Categorizes the page type (e.g. settings, dashboard, checkout) from user queries and style guide attributes.

nodes.name: search.py
nodes.summary: Executable command-line interface that exposes BM25 keyword search, stack-specific guideline search, and design system generation features.

nodes.name: format_output
nodes.summary: Formats raw search query results from CSV databases into neat Markdown snippets for terminal presentation.

nodes.name: sha256

nodes.name: supabase.ts

nodes.name: main.tsx

nodes.name: _contributing.md
nodes.summary: This document provides guidelines for creating effective Postgres best practice references that work well with AI agents and LLMs.

nodes.name: _sections.md
nodes.summary: This file defines the rule categories for Postgres best practices. Rules are automatically assigned to sections based on their filename prefix.

nodes.name: _template.md
nodes.summary: A template defining the structure (including YAML frontmatter, incorrect/correct examples, and references) for new rule contributions.

nodes.name: advanced-full-text-search.md
nodes.summary: LIKE with wildcards cannot use indexes. Full-text search with tsvector is orders of magnitude faster.

nodes.name: advanced-jsonb-indexing.md
nodes.summary: JSONB queries without indexes scan the entire table. Use GIN indexes for containment queries.

nodes.name: conn-idle-timeout.md
nodes.summary: Idle connections waste resources. Configure timeouts to automatically reclaim them.

nodes.name: conn-limits.md
nodes.summary: Too many connections exhaust memory and degrade performance. Set limits based on available resources.

nodes.name: conn-pooling.md
nodes.summary: Postgres connections are expensive (1-3MB RAM each). Without pooling, applications exhaust connections under load.

nodes.name: conn-prepared-statements.md
nodes.summary: Prepared statements are tied to individual database connections. In transaction-mode pooling, connections are shared, causing conflicts.

nodes.name: data-batch-inserts.md
nodes.summary: Individual INSERT statements have high overhead. Batch multiple rows in single statements or use COPY.

nodes.name: data-n-plus-one.md
nodes.summary: N+1 queries execute one query per item in a loop. Batch them into a single query using arrays or JOINs.

nodes.name: data-pagination.md
nodes.summary: OFFSET-based pagination scans all skipped rows, getting slower on deeper pages. Cursor pagination is O(1).

nodes.name: data-upsert.md
nodes.summary: Using separate SELECT-then-INSERT/UPDATE creates race conditions. Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT for atomic upserts.

nodes.name: lock-advisory.md
nodes.summary: Advisory locks provide application-level coordination without requiring database rows to lock.

nodes.name: lock-deadlock-prevention.md
nodes.summary: Deadlocks occur when transactions lock resources in different orders. Always acquire locks in a consistent order.

nodes.name: lock-short-transactions.md
nodes.summary: Long-running transactions hold locks that block other queries. Keep transactions as short as possible.

nodes.name: lock-skip-locked.md
nodes.summary: When multiple workers process a queue, SKIP LOCKED allows workers to process different rows without waiting.

nodes.name: monitor-explain-analyze.md
nodes.summary: EXPLAIN ANALYZE executes the query and shows actual timings, revealing the true performance bottlenecks.

nodes.name: monitor-pg-stat-statements.md
nodes.summary: pg_stat_statements tracks execution statistics for all queries, helping identify slow and frequent queries.

nodes.name: monitor-vacuum-analyze.md
nodes.summary: Outdated statistics cause the query planner to make poor decisions. VACUUM reclaims space, ANALYZE updates statistics.

nodes.name: query-composite-indexes.md
nodes.summary: Recommends creating a single composite index instead of multiple single-column indexes for queries filtering on multiple columns, prioritizing equality checks.

nodes.name: query-covering-indexes.md
nodes.summary: Explains how covering indexes with the INCLUDE clause allow Postgres to perform index-only scans and fetch data directly from the index without table heap lookups.

nodes.name: query-index-types.md
nodes.summary: Provides a guide for selecting the optimal Postgres index type (such as B-tree, GIN, GiST, BRIN, or Hash) depending on the target data type and query operators.

nodes.name: query-missing-indexes.md
nodes.summary: Highlights the critical importance of adding indexes to columns used in WHERE and JOIN clauses to prevent performance-degrading sequential scans.

nodes.name: query-partial-indexes.md
nodes.summary: Advocates the use of partial indexes with a WHERE condition to index only a subset of rows, reducing index storage size and speeding up filtered queries.

nodes.name: schema-constraints.md
nodes.summary: Explains how to add unique, check, and foreign key constraints idempotently in Postgres migrations using PL/pgSQL DO blocks to prevent syntax errors.

nodes.name: schema-data-types.md
nodes.summary: Provides schema design guidelines for selecting appropriate data types in Postgres (such as bigint, text, timestamptz, and numeric) to reduce storage and optimize comparisons.

nodes.name: schema-foreign-key-indexes.md
nodes.summary: Recommends always indexing foreign key columns in PostgreSQL because they are not indexed by default, which causes slow JOIN operations and cascading deletes.

nodes.name: schema-lowercase-identifiers.md
nodes.summary: Advises using lowercase snake_case naming conventions for database tables and columns to avoid case-sensitivity issues and the constant need for quoted identifiers.

nodes.name: schema-partitioning.md
nodes.summary: Describes table partitioning by range or list in PostgreSQL to split large tables (especially time-series data) into smaller partitions for faster queries.

nodes.name: schema-primary-keys.md
nodes.summary: Compares primary key strategies in PostgreSQL, recommending SQL-standard identity columns or time-ordered UUIDv7/ULID identifiers over serial and random UUIDv4 keys.

nodes.name: security-privileges.md
nodes.summary: Recommends creating separate read-only and write-only database roles with minimal privileges and revoking default public access to implement the principle of least privilege.

nodes.name: security-rls-basics.md
nodes.summary: Discusses using PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) to enforce data access boundaries directly at the database level, ensuring users can only query their own data.

nodes.name: security-rls-performance.md
nodes.summary: Focuses on optimizing Row Level Security (RLS) policies by wrapping helper functions in SELECT clauses for caching, using security definer functions, and indexing key lookup columns.

nodes.name: .env
nodes.summary: Configures local environment variables for the Supabase API URL and anonymous key, used by both the development server and Vite bundler.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json

layers.name: UI and Presentation Layer
layers.description: Contains React components, pages, views, stylesheets, and main UI entry points for the client interfaces.

layers.name: Services and Helpers Layer
layers.description: Handles business logic, API communication (e.g., Supabase clients), background tasks, utility helpers, and helper scripts.

layers.name: Data and Models Layer
layers.description: Defines TypeScript types, interfaces, schemas, database migrations, and static data templates like CSV files.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Manages core system operations, desktop container integration (Electron), build distributions, database connections, logging, and sync operations.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Contains build configurations, environment configurations, and dependency definitions.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Provides project documentation, guidelines, reference materials, and agent instruction files.

tour.title: Database Schema & Migrations
tour.description: Explore the SQL schemas and migrations that form the backend foundation of the clinic app. These define tables for patients, user accounts, appointments, and financial records on Supabase.
tour.languageLesson: Postgres SQL scripts declare schemas and track modifications over time. Standard relational definitions serve as the structural blueprints for the frontend models.

tour.title: Shared Data Models & Types
tour.description: This file contains the unified TypeScript interfaces representing clinic models like User, Doctor, Client, Appointment, and Finance records. It is a central dependency heavily imported throughout the codebase.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript interfaces type-check data fetched from API endpoints. Centralizing these shapes prevents mismatches between the database schema and React component states.

tour.title: Desktop Container (Electron)
tour.description: Understand how the desktop shell wrapper is configured. This layer sets up the window manager, security bounds, event-based IPC communication, and initial container boot stages.

tour.title: Local Storage & Sync Engine
tour.description: Examine how SQLite database operations and background sync mechanisms are implemented in Electron. These manage localized caching and queue data sync requests back to Supabase.

tour.title: React Bootstrapping & Layout
tour.description: Trace the main React frontend entry point which bootstraps the client application under Vite. It initializes Tailwind CSS classes and renders the primary wrapper containing global routers and context providers.

tour.title: Authentication & Branch Selection
tour.description: See how users are authenticated into the clinic workspace. The app validates user credentials via Electron IPC and queries the database for available branches for the user to choose from.

tour.title: Dashboard & Scheduling Hub
tour.description: Discover the main workspace of the application. The dashboard summarizes daily status and metrics, while the scheduling calendar allows front-desk and clinical staff to schedule, update, or cancel patient appointments.

tour.title: Patient Directory & Profiles
tour.description: Examine how clinical staff search, filter, and view the list of registered clients. The profile view coordinates histories, specific logs, and various sub-profiles for physical treatment.

tour.title: Clinical Specialty Records
tour.description: Review the multi-tab medical profile components designed for physical therapy, nutrition tracking, and lymphatic drainage. These components gather body measurements, tests, red flags, and custom exercises.

tour.title: AI Assistant Dashboard
tour.description: Explore the conversational AI chat interface built within the application. Practitioners can leverage it to ask context-specific questions about patient history, symptoms, and previous plans.

tour.title: Patient Face Portal
tour.description: Dive into the separate sub-project designed for patient self-service. Located in its own directory, it lets patients enter a PIN to log in, fill daily check-in forms, and consult assigned exercises.
